Just as land can be reclaimed, it can be forfeited too. The Turkish exclave of Ada Kaleh, a small Romanian island, was completely submerged in 1970 following the construction of a dam spanning this gorge on the Danube.

A less-intentional example of manmade flooding occurred in this country, when a landslide into the reservoir of the Vajont (“va-YONT”) Dam caused the disastrous flooding of Longarone, a town just north of Venice.
